title: tests: Replace cat with bash input redirection
body:
tests: Replace cat with bash input redirection

From the bash manpage:
> The command substitution `$(cat file)` can be replaced by the equivalent but faster `$(< file)`.

This way we avoid spawning a shell process for a simple cat.

comments.body:
> Are the test environments guaranteed to have bash? Is this redirection supported on shells such as ash or ksh?

bats needs bash :)

I meant to refer to the instances that seem to modify a Containerfile, for instance:
```
from alpine
run echo "\$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_quota_us) \$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_period_us)"
```

> I meant to refer to the instances that seem to modify a Containerfile, for instance:
> 
> ```
> from alpine
> run echo "\$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_quota_us) \$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_period_us)"
> ```

Good catch.  Fixed.  Weird that the openQA passed though.

> > I meant to refer to the instances that seem to modify a Containerfile, for instance:
> > ```
> > from alpine
> > run echo "\$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_quota_us) \$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_period_us)"
> > ```
> 
> Good catch. Fixed. Weird that the openQA passed though.

That is an an else branch on cgroupv2 so only running under cgroupv1 which we no longer support.

I guess looking at tests there is properly a lot of cleanup work still to do for the cgroupv1 removal, we dropped support for it already here https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6424 so I guess all these calls where missed?
cc @lsm5 @nalind

> > > I meant to refer to the instances that seem to modify a Containerfile, for instance:
> > > ```
> > > from alpine
> > > run echo "\$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_quota_us) \$(< /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.cfs_period_us)"
> > > ```
> > 
> > 
> > Good catch. Fixed. Weird that the openQA passed though.
> 
> That is an an else branch on cgroupv2 so only running under cgroupv1 which we no longer support.
> 
> I guess looking at tests there is properly a lot of cleanup work still to do for the cgroupv1 removal, we dropped support for it already here #6424 so I guess all these calls where missed? cc @lsm5 @nalind

err yup, and that's my bad. I'll do a followup PR for this.
